articles.summary: Turns out, CEOs have confessions  [4]to make. Dataiku has the answer.

articles.title: MISTRAL LAUNCHES AGENTS API
articles.summary: Mistral's new Agents API enables persistent, multi-agent workflows with built-in connectors for code execution, web search, RAG, image generation, and MCP support.

articles.title: OPENAI LAUNCHES "SIGN IN WITH CHATGPT"
articles.summary: OpenAI is developing a system that lets users sign into third-party apps using their ChatGPT accounts, similar to "Sign in with Google," and is seeking developer partners.

articles.title: CLAUDE'S VOICE MODE
articles.summary: Anthropic is launching a beta voice mode for Claude on mobile, enabling users to engage in voice-based tasks like summarizing calendars or searching documents.

articles.title: HOW AI IS ERODING THE NORMS OF WAR
articles.summary: Battlefield reporting reveals how AI-powered weapons are outpacing international law and creating a "race to the bottom" in warfare ethics. In Ukraine, drones are causing 70-80% of casualties, and combatants are using civilian disguises and human shields to evade AI targeting systems that struggle to distinguish between military and civilian targets.

articles.title: OAUTH FOR AGENTIC AI
articles.summary: Microsoft outlined how next-gen AI agents will require evolved identity and access standards, as it shifts from reactive assistants to proactive collaborators capable of operating autonomously across systems.

articles.title: EFFICIENT GRPO AT SCALE
articles.summary: Liger is an optimized trainer for Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) that cuts memory usage by 40% and adds FSDP and PEFT support, making reinforcement learning fine-tuning more efficient and scalable.

articles.title: GOOGLE OPEN SOURCES LMEVAL FOR CROSS-MODEL EVALUATION
articles.summary: LMEval is a framework for benchmarking AI models across different providers with multimodal support and incremental evaluation.

articles.title: LEARNING TO REASON WITHOUT EXTERNAL REWARDS
articles.summary: INTUITOR is a reinforcement learning method that uses an LLM's internal confidence level as a reward signal instead of costly domain-specific supervision. The resulting models match performance on math benchmarks while achieving superior generalization on coding tasks, offering a potential alternative to current RL methods that struggle without verifiable rewards.

articles.title: BENCHMARKING AUDIO-VISUAL QA
articles.summary: Daily-Omni is a benchmark and training-free agent for evaluating models on tasks requiring synchronized audio-visual understanding.

articles.title: FUTUREHOUSE PREVIEWS AN AI TOOL FOR 'DATA-DRIVEN' BIOLOGY DISCOVERY
articles.summary: FutureHouse's Finch is a tool for data-driven discovery in biology, simulating a "1st yr grad student." Although AI promises to automate scientific steps, evidence of breakthroughs is lacking, with Finch yet to deliver a novel discovery. FutureHouse is seeking experts to enhance Finch's accuracy during its closed beta phase.

articles.title: USING ANTHROPIC'S WEB SEARCH WITH INSTRUCTOR FOR REAL-TIME DATA
articles.summary: Anthropic's web search tool integrated with Instructor enables real-time data retrieval and structured outputs using Pydantic models. This combination allows applications to provide up-to-date information with cited sources, reducing the risk of outdated or incorrect information. Users can configure search constraints such as domain limitations, enhancing the tool's precision.

articles.title: FALLING BEHIND OPENAI AND GOOGLE, META RESTRUCTURES AI ORG
articles.summary: Meta is splitting its AI organization into two teams: AI Products, which handles Meta AI assistant and app features, and AGI Foundations, which covers Llama models and advanced capabilities.

articles.title: AMBIENCE ANNOUNCES OPENAI-POWERED MEDICAL CODING MODEL THAT OUTPERFORMS PHYSICIANS
articles.summary: Ambience Healthcare has a new medical coding model that could reduce billing mistakes and help clinicians and professional coders work more efficiently.

articles.title: MEMORY CHANGES EVERYTHING
articles.summary: ChatGPT's memory shows that AI doesn't just respond, but truly grasps patterns in how we think.
